openmeter/customer/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL implementation of customer.Adapter — the sole layer that touches the database for the customer domain. All reads and writes are wrapped in entutils.TransactingRepo so every method honors the ctx-bound Ent transaction.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every method body
patterns.description: Every exported method wraps logic in entutils.TransactingRepo (value) or TransactingRepoWithNoValue (void). Direct repo.db.* calls occur only inside the callback, never on the outer adapter receiver.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, repo *adapter) (*customer.Customer, error) { return toDomain(repo.db.Customer.Query()...) })

patterns.name: TxCreator + TxUser triad
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx() (HijackTx + NewTxDriver), WithTx() (NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self(). All three required for TransactingRepo to rebind to caller-supplied transactions.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Input validation before DB access
patterns.description: Every method calls input.Validate() and wraps errors in models.NewGenericValidationError before any DB query.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("error creating customer: %w", err)) }

patterns.name: Soft-delete pattern
patterns.description: Deletion sets DeletedAt (clock.Now().UTC()) on Customer, CustomerSubjects, AppCustomer, AppStripeCustomer, AppCustomInvoicingCustomer rows; cascade updates filter DeletedAtIsNil() to preserve earlier timestamps.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: adapter.go declares var _ customer.Adapter = (*adapter)(nil) immediately after the struct.

patterns.name: Entity mapping separated in entitymapping.go
patterns.description: All DB-to-domain conversions live in entitymapping.go. Edges must be loaded first; db.IsNotLoaded(err) must be checked.
patterns.example: subjectEntities, err := customerEntity.Edges.SubjectsOrErr(); if db.IsNotLoaded(err) { return nil, errors.New("subjects must be loaded") }

patterns.name: Domain error wrapping
patterns.description: Ent constraint errors become customer.NewKeyConflictError or models.NewGenericConflictError; zero rows become models.NewGenericNotFoundError. Raw ent errors never bubble up.

key_file_guides.role: Struct definition, Config validation, Tx/WithTx/Self triad, constructor, and compile-time interface assertion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: When adding fields to the adapter struct, clone them in WithTx; forgetting breaks ctx-propagated transactions for new fields.

key_file_guides.role: All adapter method implementations: ListCustomers, CreateCustomer, DeleteCustomer, GetCustomer, GetCustomerByUsageAttribution, UpdateCustomer, ListCustomerUsageAttributions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call repo.db.* on the outer adapter receiver — only inside a TransactingRepo callback. Always call WithSubjects() before CustomerFromDBEntity or SubjectsOrErr panics.

key_file_guides.role: Pure DB-to-domain conversion functions with no DB calls: CustomerFromDBEntity, subjectKeysFromDBEntity, resolveActiveSubscriptionIDs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Accessing Edges fields without loading them (WithSubjects, WithActiveSubscriptions) causes db.IsNotLoaded errors; check them explicitly.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db.* directly on the outer adapter receiver instead of inside a TransactingRepo callback — bypasses ctx-bound Ent transaction.
Hard-deleting customer or customer_subjects rows — the domain uses soft-delete via DeletedAt everywhere.
Adding DB query logic inside entitymapping.go — it must remain a pure conversion layer.
Returning raw Ent errors without wrapping in models.GenericNotFoundError / GenericValidationError — breaks the HTTP error-mapping chain.

decisions.decision: TransactingRepo wraps every method body rather than relying on callers to pass a transaction client.
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate implicitly via ctx; helpers using the raw *entdb.Client bypass the ctx-bound transaction and cause partial writes under concurrency.

decisions.decision: Soft-delete instead of hard-delete for customers and subjects.
decisions.rationale: Billing and subscription entities reference customers; hard deletes orphan related records and break audit trails.

decisions.decision: Entity mapping isolated in entitymapping.go with no DB access.
decisions.rationale: Pure conversion functions are independently testable and prevent accidental re-querying during domain mapping.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new adapter method that reads and writes within the same transaction

openmeter/customer/app.purpose: Thin integration contract between the customer domain and the app (marketplace) domain — defines the optional App interface (ValidateCustomer) and the AsCustomerApp type-assertion helper. No state, no DB access, no business logic.

patterns.name: Optional interface via runtime type assertion
patterns.description: AsCustomerApp performs a runtime type assertion on app.App; if the concrete type does not implement customerapp.App it returns models.NewGenericValidationError rather than panicking.
patterns.example: customerApp, ok := customerAppCandidate.(App); if !ok { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("is not a customer app [id=%s]", ...)) }

key_file_guides.role: Declares customerapp.App interface (ValidateCustomer) and AsCustomerApp helper. Zero state.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must not import openmeter/customer/adapter or openmeter/customer/service — only openmeter/app and openmeter/customer domain types.

anti_patterns: Panicking on failed type assertion instead of returning models.NewGenericValidationError.
Adding business logic, DB calls, or state to this package — it is purely an interface/assertion layer.
Importing openmeter/customer/adapter or openmeter/customer/service — creates import cycles.

decisions.decision: Separate package rather than adding ValidateCustomer to the core app.App interface.
decisions.rationale: Not all apps need customer validation; the opt-in type assertion avoids polluting the core app.App interface for every new capability.

code_examples.scenario: Type-asserting an app.App into a customer-capable app

openmeter/customer/httpdriver.purpose: v1 HTTP adapter layer for the customer API — translates api.* request/response types to customer.Service calls using the httptransport.Handler pattern. Contains no business logic; all domain decisions delegate to customer.Service, entitlement.Service, and subscription.Service.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs per endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint is a method returning a named handler type, built via httptransport.NewHandler(decoder, operation, encoder, ...options). Decoder maps request to domain input, operation calls service, encoder writes JSON.
patterns.example: func (h *handler) ListCustomers() ListCustomersHandler { return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoder, operation, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[ListCustomersResponse](http.StatusOK), httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("listCustomers"))...) }

patterns.name: Type aliases for handler request/response/handler types
patterns.description: Each endpoint block declares a type alias group (Request/Response/Handler) before the method, making each handler self-documenting and consistent with generated server stubs.
patterns.example: type ( ListCustomersResponse = pagination.Result[api.Customer]; ListCustomersRequest = customer.ListCustomersInput; ListCustomersHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[ListCustomersRequest, ListCustomersResponse, ListCustomersParams] )

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via resolveNamespace(ctx)
patterns.description: Every handler calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) which delegates to namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace. Namespace is never read from URL params directly.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ListCustomersRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: API-to-domain mapping in apimapping.go
patterns.description: All conversions between api.* types and domain types live in apimapping.go (MapCustomerCreate, CustomerToAPI, MapAddress). Handler files import these and never inline conversion.

patterns.name: Deleted-customer guard before mutations
patterns.description: UpdateCustomer, DeleteCustomer, and entitlement-value handlers call cus.IsDeleted() after fetch and return models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError if true.
patterns.example: if cus != nil && cus.IsDeleted() { return UpdateCustomerResponse{}, models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError(fmt.Errorf("customer is deleted")) }

patterns.name: Compile-time Handler interface assertion
patterns.description: handler.go declares var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) to enforce the interface at compile time.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface declaration, handler struct with injected services, New() constructor, resolveNamespace helper, and compile-time assertion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoints must be added to both the Handler interface and the implementation; the var _ assertion catches missing implementations.

key_file_guides.role: Handler method implementations for all CRUD and entitlement-value endpoints.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Deleted-customer guard must precede mutations; mapCustomerWithSubscriptionsToAPI is a shared helper used in Create, Get, and Update — keep it consistent.

key_file_guides.role: All api.* to domain type conversions. Pure functions, no service calls.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MapCustomerCreate and MapCustomerReplaceUpdate are structurally identical — keep them in sync when customer fields are added.

anti_patterns: Placing business logic (validation, conflict resolution) in decoder or encoder functions — all logic belongs in the domain service.
Reading namespace from URL/query params directly instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx).
Inline API-to-domain conversion in handler methods instead of using apimapping.go helpers.
Returning domain types (customer.Customer) directly from handlers instead of api.* types.
Calling adapter methods directly from the handler — always go through the service interface.

decisions.decision: Handler methods return typed handler values rather than implementing http.Handler directly.
decisions.rationale: httptransport.Handler provides uniform error encoding, OTel operation naming, and middleware chaining without duplicating boilerplate per method.

decisions.decision: apimapping.go is a dedicated file for API type conversions.
decisions.rationale: Keeps handler files focused on request routing; makes the API contract surface easy to audit and test in isolation.

code_examples.scenario: Define a customer endpoint handler with type aliases and resolveNamespace

openmeter/customer/testutils.purpose: Isolated test environment factory for the customer domain — constructs a fully wired CustomerService and SubjectService against a real pgtestdb PostgreSQL instance without importing app/common, preventing test-only import cycles.

patterns.name: TestEnv struct with Close via sync.Once
patterns.description: TestEnv holds all constructed services and raw DB handles. Close() uses sync.Once to safely close the Ent driver and pgx pool exactly once even from deferred teardown.

patterns.name: Build from package constructors, not app/common
patterns.description: NewTestEnv constructs customeradapter.New, customerservice.New, subjectadapter.New, subjectservice.New directly — no app/common, no Wire, no DI container.

patterns.name: eventbus.NewMock for publisher
patterns.description: Uses eventbus.NewMock(t) instead of a real Kafka publisher so tests do not require a running broker.

patterns.name: t.Context() for test-scoped context
patterns.description: All context values passed to service calls use t.Context() (not context.Background()) so cancellation ties to the test lifecycle.

patterns.name: DBSchemaMigrate before schema-dependent tests
patterns.description: Tests requiring tables must call env.DBSchemaMigrate(t) before the first service call; skipping causes 'table does not exist' errors.

key_file_guides.role: TestEnv struct, NewTestEnv factory, DBSchemaMigrate helper, and Close teardown. Single file for the entire test environment.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Close must be deferred in every test using NewTestEnv; DBSchemaMigrate must be called before any schema-dependent query.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from testutils — creates import cycles and makes tests dependent on the full DI graph.
Using context.Background() in tests instead of t.Context() — misses test-lifecycle cancellation and resource cleanup.
Calling e.Close() without defer — leaks DB connections if the test panics.
Adding business logic or assertions to testutils — keep it a pure environment factory.

decisions.decision: testutils constructs adapters and services directly instead of using app/common Wire providers.
decisions.rationale: Avoids import cycles between domain test helpers and the application wiring layer; lets domain tests compile without pulling in unrelated domain providers.

code_examples.scenario: Build a customer TestEnv from raw constructors with a mock event bus
code_examples.code:
import (
	customeradapter "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/customer/adapter"
	customerservice "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/customer/service"
	"github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/watermill/eventbus"
)

publisher := eventbus.NewMock(t)
customerAdapter, err := customeradapter.New(customeradapter.Config{Client: client, Logger: logger})
require.NoError(t, err)
svc, err := customerservice.New(customerservice.Config{Adapter: customerAdapter, Publisher: publisher})
require.NoError(t, err)
